new_terms.context: Nvidia's rack form factor for Rubin GPU systems, similar to the Blackwell NVL72 rack.
Short for bidirectional, used in the context of electrical lane speed (e.g., 224Gbit/s bi-di).
Refers to the physical edge of a chip die where I/O connections are made, critical for bandwidth density.
The currently approved and official technical plan or configuration for a product.
The component in a CPO system that converts electrical signals to optical signals.
A network configuration where every node or rack has a direct connection to every other node.
Refers to interconnects using copper cables/traces instead of optical fibers.
